name: Interpretation Forecast review for Circulating Factor Modulation bundle
summary: The staged interpretation is conservative and forecast-safe. The outlook keeps evidence_stage, momentum, and confidence unchanged; confines human functional support to Alzheimer disease plasma exchange; describes healthy-adult plasma-exchange evidence as mixed and biomarker-level; and requires replicated functional, intrinsic-capacity, durability, disease, or mortality endpoints before confidence or broad-aging interpretation can increase.

findings.claim_or_issue: The staged outlook preserves human_functional_benefit only as a narrow Alzheimer disease-specific plasma-exchange boundary.
findings.why_it_matters: Without that wording, readers could infer that the track has broad healthy-aging functional evidence in humans.
findings.recommended_action: Retain the staged interpretation note and stage-assignment support evidence tying functional benefit to AMBAR and the historical-control Alzheimer cohort only.

findings.claim_or_issue: The staged outlook describes healthy-adult TPE and plasmapheresis evidence as mixed biomarker evidence instead of upgrading the track based on the favorable randomized multi-omics paper.
findings.why_it_matters: The repaired map includes both positive biomarker movement and a limiting plasmapheresis epigenetic-clock result.
findings.recommended_action: Keep the mixed-biomarker framing and low confidence rating.

findings.claim_or_issue: The rating-change criteria require replicated functional or intrinsic-capacity endpoints and clearer mechanism attribution before any stronger broad-aging read.
findings.why_it_matters: This prevents no-results registries, preprint feasibility signals, review-level sources, and short-term biomarker movement from being interpreted as forecast progress.
findings.recommended_action: Retain the staged rating-change criteria and ordinary-surveillance next mode.
